title: Add 'P' (pipeline) arm to benchmark suite for shipped verification mode
context: hunch v1.4.1+ introduced a production verification pipeline that runs headless with hooks from settings.json, without skills or the hunch graph. Need to benchmark this new operational mode alongside existing test configurations.
decision: Extended ARMS and makeWorktree to support arm='P', which initializes worktrees via 'hunch init --firmness firm --no-index --no-enforce --no-providers', enabling tests of the shipped pipeline verification mode in isolation from graph and skill systems.
consequences: Benchmark suite now covers three independent test configurations: bare (A), +cold hunch graph (C), +fable-mode skill (S), and +shipped pipeline (P)
Pipeline arm validates that production verification flow works correctly on real tasks (zod-5842, zod-5296 test results included)
Establishes a testable path for the new minimal headless pipeline mode without requiring full hunch infrastructure
alternatives_rejected: Reuse existing arm (e.g., test pipeline under arm 'C') — loses isolation and conflates graph vs. pipeline behavior
Test pipeline mode outside the benchmark harness — misses comparative performance and behavior data
Embed pipeline config directly in worktree setup — using 'hunch init' as the initialization vector is idiomatic and explicit

rejected_tripwires.alternative: Reuse existing arm (e.g., test pipeline under arm 'C') — loses isolation and conflates graph vs. pipeline behavior
Test pipeline mode outside the benchmark harness — misses comparative performance and behavior data
Embed pipeline config directly in worktree setup — using 'hunch init' as the initialization vector is idiomatic and explicit
